Feature Request #136943: Disable option for SearchBar

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Sometimes, a user interface element is inappropriate/not applicable for the current program conditions and it would be confusing to entire hide this element. The best alternative is being able to disable the element. Flutter provides this capability for some widgets by passing null to the onPressed.

[SearchBar](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/SearchBar-class.html) is a widget utilized by some third party plugins such as [simple_search_dropdown](https://pub.dev/packages/simple_search_dropdown). I can find no way to disable its widget. However, since it utilizes searchbar, it would be good if searchbar could provide an "enabled" parameter. Passing false to this widget would cause it to ignore mouse clicks, tabbing into it and have it display with a grayed out look to let the user know it's disabled. 

### Proposal

Explained above.

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #137251: Implement similar widget to``AnimatedDefaultTextStyle`` but for child ``Icon``

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I have a ``Row``with a ``Text``and an ``Icon`` as children. I'm using ``AnimateDefaultTextStyle`` to animate the color change and the fontWeight change on the text, but I want to apply that same color animation that the text has, to the icon. 

### Proposal

``AnimateDefaultTextStyle`` should also affect to child ``Icon``. Based on the use case I commented, If I want to also animate the color change on the icon, apart of the text, I have to create an animation controller and the animation just for the icon, because the text does that with the ``AnimateDefaultTextStyle``.
The alternative would be to create a widget similar to ``AnimateDefaultTextStyle`` but for ``Icon``.

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #137268: `flutter run --local-engine` should generate overridden dependencies for `sky_engine` automatically.

Right now, when I modify Dart code in my local engine build and want to test those with flutter code from the flutter repo, I run something like this:

```
flutter run -d linux --local-engine-host=host_debug_unopt --local-engine=host_debug_unopt
```

The problem is that this fails to compile because the local version of sky_engine doesn't match the Dart changes I've made in the engine, and I need to add something like this to the `pubspec.yaml` of the thing I am running:

```yaml
dependency_overrides:
  sky_engine:
    path: /usr/local/home/gspencer/code/engine/src/out/host_debug_unopt/gen/dart-pkg/sky_engine
```

Making these changes now works for running the app I want to run, but the IDE still shows errors until I also add that same stanza to the `packages/flutter/pubspec.yaml` file.

Which means I need to update the override if I switch to another build type, and I need to remember to revert the pubspecs before committing.

It wouldn't fix the IDE problem, but when I specify `--local-engine` to `flutter run`, it would make sense to also transparently add the correct override to the pubspec for running the application.  It might mean that it somehow needs to...

Status: shipped/closed

The problem is that this fails to compile because the local version of sky_engine doesn't match the Dart changes I've made in the engine, and I need to add something like this to the `pubspec.yaml` of the thing I am running:

```yaml
dependency_overrides:
  sky_engine:
    path: /usr/local/home/gspencer/code/engine/src/out/host_debug_unopt/gen/dart-pkg/sky_engine
```

Making these changes now works for running the app I want to run, but the IDE still shows errors until I also add that same stanza to the `packages/flutter/pubspec.yaml` file.

Which means I need to update the override if I switch to another build type, and I need to remember to revert the pubspecs before committing.

It wouldn't fix the IDE problem, but when I specify `--local-engine` to `flutter run`, it would make sense to also transparently add the correct override to the pubspec for running the application.  It might mean that it somehow needs to...

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #137311: Clean up memory leaks in Flutter Framework, phase 2.

Looking for **volunteers** to help with this!!! 🚀

Issues: [all](https://github.com/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3A%22a%3A+leak+tracking%22+), [open](https://github.com/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22a%3A+leak+tracking%22).
PRs: [all](https://github.com/pulls?q=is%3Apr+label%3A%22a%3A+leak+tracking%22+), [open](https://github.com/pulls?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Apr+archived%3Afalse+label%3A%22a%3A+leak+tracking%22+).


Focus areas: 
@polina-c: kick off cleanup for notGCed and [integration with testWidgets](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bXannXQ8rEtfwxEVd7LDo7xiVu5-QxRoall28MIIFss/edit#heading=h.dk8qogiupft4)


Bigger issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/134787

# Goal of this issue 
1. All disposables in FlutterFramework (listed below) should be instrumented for leak tracking ([example](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/137309)).
2. There should not be `.withIgnored(` under [flutter/test](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/tree/master/packages/flutter/test) (or `.withIgnored(` should have a comment that explains why the ignored leaks are ok).

Leak tracking is now off by default for Flutter Framework.

Feature Request #137435: Stop duplicating code across disposables.

We are duplicating code across disposables in order to instrument code for leak tracking by communicating creation and disposal to MemoryAllocations.instance

Design draft: http://flutter.dev/go/introduce-disposable

When you duplicate the code again, leave TODO:

// TODO(polina-c): stop duplicating code across disposables

text:
Feature Request #137530: ExpansionTile Improvements

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

There is no problem indeed but this would be much better if the `ExpansionTile` widget supports these parameters:
* dense
* visualDensity
* reverse (if it is `true` then the tile should render beneath its children) - defaults to `false`
* enabled
* enableFeedback

> I know that we can wrap this widget with ExpansionTileTheme or even ListTileTheme to force changes but it would be much easier to have direct access to those parameters in the main widget.

### Proposal

I want to add more access to the ExpansionTile widget's ListTile and make it possible to change the arrangement of placing ListTile and the children.

text:
Feature Request #137608: [shared_preferences] Support namespaces/prefixes for multi-account apps

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I have an app that supports multiple accounts and want separate `SharedPreferences` per account.

### Proposal

If an app supports multiple accounts and wants to use separate preferences per account the current `shared_preferences` implementation does not suffice.

I suggest allowing `SharedPreferences.setPrefix()` to be allowed to be called multiple times (currently it throws an error) and to invalidate the cache each time `setPrefix()` is called.

This would allow multi-account apps to separate preferences per account.

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #137683: Add support for fixed accent colors

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

[Part of #91605 ]
Material 3 has introduced a set of new color roles that retain the same color in the light and dark themes collectively referred to as fixed accent colors See: https://m3.material.io/styles/color/the-color-system/color-roles#232e7275-6ef2-4b93-afa6-c5f76e378ced

New surface color roles have also been added: (See #137679)
These changes appear to be part of a wider set of updates to the color system, see [this](https://youtu.be/vnDhq8W98O4?t=649). 


### Color Roles

#### Fixed Accent Colors
See: https://m3.material.io/styles/color/the-color-system/color-roles#232e7275-6ef2-4b93-afa6-c5f76e378ced
| Color Role         | Tone: Light | Tone: Dark |
| ------------------ | ----------- | ---------- |
| Primary Fixed      | P-90        | P-90       |
| Primary Fixed Dim  | P-80        | P-80       |
| Secondary Fixed    | S-90        | S-90       |
| Secondary Fixed Dim| S-80        | S-80       |
| Tertiary Fixed     |...

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #137721: [go_router] Add ability use query parameters during redirection with go_router

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I need to pass "from" as a query to the route. After everything I needed will be done on that route I want to redirect back to the uri from route parameter. Now it doesn't work, but I'm pretty sure it was working like this before. For example here's the logs: 
```
[GoRouter] getting location for name: "verifyPhoneNumber", queryParameters: {from: /join/hoopit-746087/open/lukas-sitt-lag-385239/roles}

```
And as you can see in the second line no info about query parameters has been passed. If I missing something, please let me know, but so far seem like unreasonable restriction

I propose add/return ability to pass query params to redirection callback

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the attached demo app
2. Horizontally scroll the text in the dropdown menu

### Expected results

I would like to have an option which disables horizontal scrolling of the text.
When i limit the width of the dropdown menu i am aware that menu item's labels can have a larger text and therefore are not fully visible.
At the moment it seems that the text is always scrollable when i drag it horizontally.
Since i have to horizontally scroll some DropdownMenu instances in a Row the scrolling of an overflowing text inside a menu at the same time is quite disturbing.

### Actual results

When horizontally dragging the overflowing text of a dropdown menu it is always scrolling.

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

const Color darkBlue = Color.fromARGB(255, 18, 32, 47);

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
 ...

Feature Request #137985: [DropdownMenu] Able to disable horizontal scrolling for the selected menu

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the attached demo app
2. Horizontally scroll the text in the dropdown menu

### Expected results

I would like to have an option which disables horizontal scrolling of the text.
When i limit the width of the dropdown menu i am aware that menu item's labels can have a larger text and therefore are not fully visible.
At the moment it seems that the text is always scrollable when i drag it horizontally.
Since i have to horizontally scroll some DropdownMenu instances in a Row the scrolling of an overflowing text inside a menu at the same time is quite disturbing.

### Actual results

When horizontally dragging the overflowing text of a dropdown menu it is always scrolling.

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

const Color darkBlue = Color.fromARGB(255, 18, 32, 47);

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
 ...

It results in:

text:
Feature Request #138092: Add an event to support post-submit actions when all post-submit tests finish

It is not uncommon that we need to do post-submit actions when all tests finish. For example:
1) Packages repo needs to [waits for all post-submit tests to pass](https://github.com/flutter/plugins/blob/main/.github/workflows/release.yml#L37-L47) before publishing @stuartmorgan 
2) Engine repo needs to roll latest version to framework when all post-submit tests pass (WIP) @XilaiZhang 

We need to have a generic way to flag when all post-submit tests pass. Eg. this event can then be consumed from GitHub actions to act accordingly.

/cc @godofredoc 

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #138147: No offline check when performing various Flutter CLI steps

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Our attempts to onboard Flutter into a major bank conflicts with our regulatory control procedures. We cannot use any applications which will go out to the public Internet - our networks do not allow this. 

Installing Flutter via git repository is a non-starter.

We begin with the Flutter SDK packages [available here](https://docs.flutter.dev/release/archive), and set the developers environment up thus

* PUB_HOSTED_URL set to our internal Pub server
* FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL set to our internal Google mirror
* Remove the Git origin remote
* Disable telemetry
* Disable analytics

As we do this, the calls to disable telemetry and disable analytics have long network timeouts associated with them before the command concludes.

The runner does not accept --offline or --no-version-check

```shell
/bin/flutter -vv --no-version-check --disable-telemetry
[   +5 ms] executing: sw_vers -productName
[  +30 ms] Exit code 0 from:...

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #138171: Corporate users need a way to populate an internal mirror of FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

Think of some large, highly regulated companies in the US as operating in a restricted Internet capacity (even more restrictive than China).

Our developer laptops do not have free access to the Internet, nor are we allowed to poke holes in our firewall for an exception to Google servers.

When Flutter is executed, depending on the command and the OS and chipset, Flutter will go out to a network location (base url specified by `FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL`) to download any number of support files and binaries specific to that version of Flutter. 

Within a firewall (corporate or China), we have the ability to change the base URL specified by FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL to a reachable URL for the current network. Any calls made by Flutter (and a number of other tools), will respect the environment variable to locate files.

HOWEVER, without the ability to mirror Google's default base URL for supplementary Flutter content, we...

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #138208: Gap between widgets in Column widget

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I'm from web development background and in CSS we have `gap` property in flex boxes which allow developers to maintain gap with just single line.

```css
.container {
 display: flex;
 flex-direction: column;
 gap: 12px;
}
```

i'm trying to develop a mobile app with Flutter (btw really really amazing framework) and i already searched over internet there's like `gap` feature in Flutter.

### Proposal

i know that we can do that with spaceBox widget and with some other tricks but it will be a good to have a gap feature in Flutter itself.

Thank you.

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #138430: Allow (on demand/toggle) running post-submits as pre-submits

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing infra issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3Ateam-infra)

### Type of Request

feature request

### Infrastructure Environment

I'd like to see this implemented in the code that parses and/or understands `.ci.yaml`, which, IIRC, is Cocoon?

---

Similar requests:

- @gaaclarke's https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/111418.

### What is happening?

## Background

The engine repo has insufficient capacity to run every kind of test on every kind of platform on every commit.

As a result, we run many tests as [`presubmit: false`](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/main/.ci.yaml), i.e.: [this config](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/eba757803a6f3144d4875c54db51a0add551c72c/.ci.yaml#L245-L248). That works fine for the vast majority of commits, but sometimes we are changing things that are _only_ exhaustively tested by postsubmit.

Two recent examples:

1. https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/138381; 
  due to not running the clang-tidy postsubmits.

2. https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/47397;
  caused a device lab failure due...

Community support: 1 reactions (0 upvotes)

text:
Feature Request #138483: expose kexboardType and textInputAction parameters of the SearchAnchor TextField

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

For a new Material3 project, I am trying to implement SearchAnchor instead of SearchDelegate. 
However, in contrast to SearchDelegate, the new SearchAnchor appears to be limited in customization options. 
To allow for more customization of the search experience, it would be beneficial to expose parameters similar to the ones of the SearchDelegate. Example parameters are keyboardType or the textInputAction. 


### Proposal

introduce textinputAction and kexboardType as parameters for the SearchBar constructor. Also include them in SearchAnchor() and SearchAnchor.bar() constructors.

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #138511: [cocoon] Throttle swarming presubmit tasks during peak hours of users consuming a large amount of infra quota

Occasionally, someone will send a large number of cleanup PRs which consumes a large chunk of presubmit capacity. To protect overall SLOs for the community, we should deprioritize this user for the day.

Peak hours would be around 10 AM - 3 PM PST on US workdays.

This could be some metric based on the number of builds being scheduled. For example, we could batch it in groups of 50 targets. These numbers can be tweaked to better match our infra capacity:

0 - 150: No impact
150 - 250: Slight reduction
250+: Lowest priority

Cocoon can leave a comment on the PR as an FYI that the user is being throttled for using too much capacity.

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #138992: Build hook for Flutter Web?

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Hi thanks for the great framework! When developing https://github.com/fzyzcjy/flutter_rust_bridge v2, we are having the following scenario: We need to compile some Rust code into WASM, which is used by Flutter code.

For the native platforms, the build can be invoked easily. For example, in Android, we can just modify the build.gradle and let it invoke the build commands; similar for iOS, windows, macos, linux. However, IMHO there is no place to inject such "hooks" when building.

Surely, we can just create another tool that wraps the "build rust + call flutter run" process (that's what we are doing currently) - but that looks suboptimal.

I guess it is somehow related to the native assets https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/issues/50565 / https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/129757, but this is just a (small) part of what native asset system is going to cover, and seems to be also possibly orthogonal (e.g. today's Android/iOS/... build...

Community support: 11 reactions (11 upvotes)

text:
Feature Request #139012: Support `DismissDirection` in `SnackBarThemeData`

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Currently, `SnackBar` supports `DismissDirection` as a widget parameter. However it is not supported in `SnackBarThemeData` and cannot be added to the overall app theme. For comparison, `SnackBarBehavior` can be passed in both `SnackBar` and `SnackBarThemeData`.

This means whenever I create a SnackBar, I need to explicitely specify the `DismissDirection` in every SnackBar instantiation when it would be easier to just define in my app theme. 

### Proposal

Add a parameter for specifying `DismissDirection` in `SnackBarThemeData`.

```
SnackBarThemeData(
    dismissDirection: DismissDirection.horizontal,
)
```

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #139012: Support `DismissDirection` in `SnackBarThemeData`

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Currently, `SnackBar` supports `DismissDirection` as a widget parameter. However it is not supported in `SnackBarThemeData` and cannot be added to the overall app theme. For comparison, `SnackBarBehavior` can be passed in both `SnackBar` and `SnackBarThemeData`.

This means whenever I create a SnackBar, I need to explicitely specify the `DismissDirection` in every SnackBar instantiation when it would be easier to just define in my app theme. 

### Proposal

Add a parameter for specifying `DismissDirection` in `SnackBarThemeData`.

```
SnackBarThemeData(
    dismissDirection: DismissDirection.horizontal,
)
```

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #139350: Custom install fonts and other files

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Is it possible to do a local installation of all external files that are downloaded from the server, to your server, or directly to the asset?
This is necessary for two cases:
1) If you need to install the site (web version) on a secure network without access to the external Internet
2) Long time downloading a file from the server. If you put the file on the local network, the download will probably happen faster.

[Example file](https://fonts.gstatic.com/s/notosanssc/v36/k3kCo84MPvpLmixcA63oeAL7Iqp5IZJF9bmaG9_FnYxNbPzS5HE.ttf
)
Here you can see that this file takes quite a long time to download, which leads to a long loading time for the site.

![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/92320184/2dfcb19c-eba1-453c-998d-06f4017c61fe)


### Proposal

Local install fonts

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Go router currently provides the StatefulShellRoute.indexedStack() constructor. It works well when you are working with any index-based navbar and want to keep the widget state. But there is no clean way to dispose or reload the state once you are going to another location / coming back to the old location. The lack of methods to dispose the state is a problem with big apps especially.

In order to achieve a clean dispose-architecture, you can use the normal ShellRoute, but when working with a NavigationRail e.g. you have much boilerplate code to manage for initial selection, pushing routes if the selection changes and so on. 

### Proposal

A constructor like "ShellRoute.indexed()" would be great for this use case, with getters like in "StatefulShellRoute.indexedStack()" (getter for index and the ability to go to a route with an index).

If I'm overlooking another way of achieving this in a clean manner, please let me know. 

If needed, I...

text:
Feature Request #139395: Improve and fix MenuAnchor/MenuItemButton UX for desktop

MenuAnchor for Material 3 is great for contextual menus but there are some minor issues related to accessibility. Specifically, keyboard navigation does not auto-select an element when opening the menu. This causes some UX confusion where the user has to navigate further to access the menu he or she just opened, which in my case is not user friendly.

Setup:
- Material 3
- Windows 10 22H2
- Flutter 3.16.2

Relevant PR:
- [ ] https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/139396 MenuItemButton doesn't have an `autofocus` attribute, thereby making it harder to navigate with the keyboard. 


Thanks Flutter Team!

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #139692: [macOS] Add mouse cursor countingUpHand, countingDownHand, and countingUpAnDownHand

All system mouse cursors from macOS's `CoreCursor` are planned to be included except for the following three:

* kCountingUpHandCursor
* kCountingDownHandCursor
* kCountingUpAndDownHandCursor

These cursors look like a hand counting with fingers, animating from 1 to 5 or backwards. 

There are two reason they are not included:
* During testing I found that they do not animate unless the mouse is moving. (I might be wrong, since my testing app is throwing errors.)
* They're exclusively present on macOS and I'm not aware of any uses.

If you find this cursor necessary, comment below with a use case (or better, a name) and we can discuss adding this cursor.

Related:
* https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/61397

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #139929: Deeplinking web validator (Android)

A deep link is a special type of URL that takes users directly to a specific page or section within an app. It contains a scheme, host, and path to open the app and navigate to the desired location. 

Deep links allow end-users to easily navigate to the relevant content in the mobile app from a mobile website. Because mobile apps generally provide better user experience, users who’re directed to an app are twice more likely to convert (e.g. make a purchase) than users who are directed to the mobile web. 

However, deeplinking setup is complicated and often error-prone. We hope to improve this deeplinking implementation experience by first providing a validator to help developers determine if their implementation is accurate. 

In Flutter applications, a deeplink setup consists of app setup and web setup. Assuming a developer starts with a fresh Flutter application, the app setup includes the adding certain settings or tags in native Android/iOS files and the dart code to handle incoming urls. The web setup involves maintaining a website that hosts a file to associate its web domain to a flutter app.

This particular issue tracks the web setup validation for Android app...

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #140271: TapRegion has no clear way to differentiate between different gestures

### Use case

By default, the on screen keyboard is dismissed by non-mobile touch events, such as a click.
It is quite common to want similar behavior on mobile. That can be done by calling `unfocus` on the text fields `FocusNode` inside the `onTapCallback`. That does however mean that both taps and scrolls are handled the same, since `TapRegion` uses `PointerDownEvent` as a trigger for `onTapOutside`. (Contradictory to what the name might suggest. Compare with `onTap` and `onTapDown` in `GestureDetector`)

Having the ability to differentiate between scroll and tap would give developers the ability to close the text field on a tap, but keep it open when scrolling.

Attached below is a video of the Google Home app on Android, showing the behavior that I'm looking for.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/47989573/77d26c16-5359-44ae-8002-8e30ff88f8e8

### Proposal

`TapRegion`, should have more parameters to differentiate between different gestures, similar to `GestureDetector`. This would be a breaking change, but `onTapOutside` should behave like `onTap` of `GestureDetector`, and a new parameter with the name `onTapDownOutside` should be added that has the current...

Community support: 4 reactions (4 upvotes)

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #140599: Show scrollbar on 2d scroll in ListView without Width

### Use case

this use case is mainly on the web and desktop,
let's say you have a 2D scroll and on the vertical scroll you have a wild row 
what to expect is the scrollbar should be visible on the edge of the  vertical scroll  not at the end of it 
so you don't need to slide horizontally to see the vertical scrollbar:

Here is an example:
Btw I can't just make 2D scroll to listview without giving it a width

```
    return SingleChildScrollView(
      scrollDirection: Axis.horizontal,
      child: SizedBox(
        width: 3000,
        child: ListView(
          children: [
            for (var i = 0; i < 100; i++)
              Padding(
                padding: const EdgeInsets.all(8.0),
                child: Container(
                  height: 100,
                  width: 100,
                  color: [Colors.red, Colors.green, Colors.blue][i % 3],
                  child: Text("data $i"),
                ),
              ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
```


### Proposal


https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/36929077/4e25e37a-bff2-40d4-9f41-8d9031746851


The vertical should be visible without the need to scroll horizontally

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #140622: Rename MemoryAllocations to FlutterMemoryAllocations.

**Why?**

Disposables in Dart cannot use MemoryAllocations in Flutter. So, to be leak trackable they need a dart-only class.
See detains in [the document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ireWmX4J5Jc6oDO1PmAXPlLdiAlCHvTz8SVC_Sg9KM8/edit?resourcekey=0-yODb_SAStMgMefYpQeX8uQ#bookmark=id.e3vlg7f7t1l).

**Why now?**

1. We want early adopters of leak tracker (for example, package owners) to use the class with the right name, so that they do not deal with renaming later.
2. It will take time for the renaming to land to flutter stable.

**Steps:**

- [x] Add FlutterMemoryAllocations and deprecate MemoryAllocations in Flutter
- [x] Document breaking change in Flutter: https://github.com/flutter/website/pull/10021
- [x] Update leak tracker to use FlutterMemoryAllocations
- [x] Roll leak tracker to G3
- [x] Upgrade flutter to new leak tracker
- [ ] Remove MemoryAllocations from Flutter


Community support: 2 reactions (2 upvotes)

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #140656: Display information-type of each column in `flutter emulators` command.

### Use case

There can be a table-header which can show what type of information each column represents. Just for better understanding.

### Proposal

Here's the output of a `flutter emulators` command. There can be a table-header which can show what type of information each column represents.

### Before

```
4 available emulators:

apple_ios_simulator      • iOS Simulator          • Apple  • ios
Pixel_2_API_34        • Pixel 2 API 34         • Google • android
Pixel_7_Pro_API_34      • Pixel 7 Pro API 34       • Google • android
Resizable_Experimental_API_33 • Resizable (Experimental) API 33 • Generic • android
```

### After

```
4 available emulators:

ID • Name • Manufacturer • Platform Type

apple_ios_simulator      • iOS Simulator          • Apple  • ios
Pixel_2_API_34        • Pixel 2 API 34         • Google • android
Pixel_7_Pro_API_34      • Pixel 7 Pro API 34       • Google • android
Resizable_Experimental_API_33 • Resizable (Experimental) API 33 • Generic • android
```

---

Similar Issue for `flutter devices` command: #138983

text:
Feature Request #140692: [l10n] Allow `l10n.yaml` to specify line length formatting

### Use case

Hey Flutter Team, 

`flutter run` is breaking our localization files formatting regardless of configuration in `l10n.yaml`. We're using line length 100 in our project. 


- Having `format: true` in `l10n.yaml` will format to line length 80 (default). 
- Having `format: false` in `l10n.yaml` will format to full line length (no boundry) 

We're checking in localization into source code. And unfortunately there is no way to specify the line length that the generated localization files should be in. 

### Proposal

Add a flag to specify line length in  `l10n.yaml`

Community support: 2 reactions (2 upvotes)

Status: shipped/closed

text:
With content-hashing, a given git hash is _no longer_ guaranteed to provide an engine build.

For example, [Linux flutter_release_builder / 1007](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/dart-internal/builders/flutter/Linux%20flutter_release_builder/1007/overview) correctly skipped building an engine as unnecessary, as a previous builder built the same (content-hashed) engine. However, this is in conflict with [how we provide `FLUTTER_PREBUILT_ENGINE_VERSION=`, at least in presubmits](https://github.com/flutter/cocoon/blob/b88be3ecf4e49a5a8138940cc2e91e72ba6ca84e/app_dart/lib/src/service/luci_build_service.dart#L286-L293).

From talking to @jtmcdole, we should just be able to stop providing this, at least ... for release builders? Need to double check.

Plan Item #174780: Add optional MenuController parameter to DropdownMenu

### Use case

I want to be able to open/close menu of `DropdownMenu` manually.

### Proposal

Add optional `MenuController` parameter to `DropdownMenu`.  

Currently, in `DropdownMenu` state, the variable `MenuController _controller` is assigned with `MenuController()`.  
Make it `widget.menuController ?? MenuController()`.

1. `flutter create my_app`
2. `cd my_app`
3. `et run --config ios_debug_sim_unopt_arm64`
4. Quit the app once it launches
5. Open `my_app/ios/Runner.xcworkspace`
6. In Xcode do Product > Run 

Note: Error does not always happen since it's a race condition, you may need to do step 6 multiple times. It is more likely to happen if you [setup UIScene in your Info.plist](https://docs.flutter.dev/release/breaking-changes/uiscenedelegate#info-plist-migration), but I have seen it happen for both. Also, the crash can also happen when running from the Flutter CLI, but running from Xcode is faster and allows you to see the error log.

text:
App crashes

```
[FATAL:flutter/shell/platform/darwin/ios/platform_view_ios.mm(156)] Check failed: accessibility_bridge_.
```

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7a13e1be3a98704f91acf2807aede12277771bf6/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/darwin/ios/platform_view_ios.mm#L154

I tried tracing it down and it appears that occasionally `FlutterEngine` enables semantics before the `_handleFrameworkSemanticsEnabledChanged` listener is set up.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7a13e1be3a98704f91acf2807aede12277771bf6/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/darwin/ios/framework/Source/FlutterEngine.mm#L345-L351

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7a13e1be3a98704f91acf2807aede12277771bf6/packages/flutter/lib/src/semantics/binding.dart#L42

And therefore, the semantics tree is never enabled:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7a13e1be3a98704f91acf2807aede12277771bf6/packages/flutter/lib/src/semantics/binding.dart#L168-L170


Likely caused by https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/174163.

text:
Plan Item #175090: Add documentation to `DropdownMenu.onSelected` and add appropriate type constraints

https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/onSelected.html

> The callback is called when a selection is made.
>
> Defaults to null. If null, only the text field is updated.

Upon reading the field / class docs, it does not tell me what `null` represents in `T?` and when I would get `null` in the callback.

Also since `null` seems to have a special meaning to the `DropdownMenu` implementation, the class needs an additional type constraint: `T extends Object` so the user can't assign a different meaning to `null`.

text:
Plan Item #175212: [flutter/packages] clang-format is formatting c++ headers as objc

When working on packages in flutter/packages, the `format` tool incorrectly applies Objective-C formatting rules to C++ `.h` files

### Root Cause
File extension ambiguity: Both C++ and Objective-C use `.h` files

### Current Configuration

.clang-format file:
  ```yaml
  BasedOnStyle: Google
  ---
  Language: Cpp
  DerivePointerAlignment: false
  PointerAlignment: Left
  ---
  Language: ObjC
  DerivePointerAlignment: false
  PointerAlignment: Right
```

### Expected vs Actual Behavior

- Expected: C++ `.h` files formatted with PointerAlignment: Left (e.g., ```int* ptr```)
- Actual: C++ `.h` files formatted with PointerAlignment: Right (e.g., ```int *ptr```)

### To Reproduce

```sh
dart run script/tool/bin/flutter_plugin_tools.dart format --packages file_selector
```

### Possible Solution

A possible solution could be add a package-specific `.clang-format` files with C++ configuration only in packages containing C++ code

text:
Plan Item #175251: Use TextPainter built-in API for ellipsis in Slider

### Use case

For the context, check https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/173324#issuecomment-3269160721.

I'm currently appending ellipses to multiline text in Slider label. Meanwhile, TextPaint supports ellipsis in built-in API:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/844edbcd91ba0aa7b1b2d8d809689c69ebeb7222/packages/flutter/lib/src/painting/text_painter.dart#L608

However, when maxLines is set, it's not working. This is blocked by https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/50168.  Once https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/50168 is fixed, we should use built-in API instead of appending ellipses manually.

### Proposal

Use TextPainter built-in API for ellipsis in Slider. https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/173324 is followed by this.

I'm currently appending ellipses to multiline text in Slider label. Meanwhile, TextPaint supports ellipsis in built-in API:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/844edbcd91ba0aa7b1b2d8d809689c69ebeb7222/packages/flutter/lib/src/painting/text_painter.dart#L608

However, when maxLines is set, it's not working. This is blocked by https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/50168.  Once https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/50168 is fixed, we should use built-in API instead of appending ellipses manually.

### Proposal

Use TextPainter built-in API for ellipsis in Slider. https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/173324 is followed by this.

Plan Item #175516: [Google3 Bug]: ImageStream & ImageProvider provide no way to pause GIF animations

Related: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/130976

### Help us understand the severity of this issue

- [ ] causing severe production issues e.g. malfunctions or data loss
- [ ] blocking next binary release
- [x] blocking a client feature launch within a quarter
- [ ] nice-to-have but does not block a launch within the next quarter

### Steps to reproduce

1. Load a GIF asset into `ImageProvider`/`ImageStream` via `CachedNetworkImage`.

### Expected results

Either one of the following:

1. The `ImageProvider`/`ImageStream` library provides a way to disable any animation of the provided image.
2. `MediaQuery.of(context).disableAnimations` is respected by the `ImageProvider`/`ImageStream` library by default.

### Actual results

1.  Neither `ImageProvider`/`ImageStream` provide a way to disable animation of an image
2. `MediaQuery.of(context).disableAnimations` is not respected by the `ImageProvider`/`ImageStream` library.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

yaml:
```xml
dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  cached_network_image: ^3.3.1 # Use the latest version
```


Dart File:
```dart
import 'package:cached_network_image/cached_network_image.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'GIF Loading with CachedNetworkImage',
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(title: const Text('Cached GIF')),
        body: Center(
          child: CachedNetworkImage(
            imageUrl:
                'https://media1.tenor.com/m/Ps_NnXFTyRYAAAAC/send-link-send.gif',
            placeholder: (context, url) => const CircularProgressIndicator(),
            errorWidget: (context, url, error) => const Icon(Icons.error),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

Plan Item #175607: [`Flutter News Template`] toolkit : Can we see the template brick , so that we can extend that too.

### Use case

I can see that the `Flutter News Template` toolkit and the brick used to scaffold the project is the shell script and it is using the brickhub.dev link to download the template file. 
Is it possible to opensource that also so we can extend using that? There can be many things that can be contributed to that project.

eg. Adding goRouter,  changing block to riverpod, using packages that are complementing firebase set up etc.

### Proposal

It has to be first done from flutter side and later we can add bricks to help improve that.

I hope my thoughts are clear and would be open to have conversation on this.

Plan Item #175655: remove "This trailing comma makes auto-formatting nicer for build methods" from flutter create template

### Steps to reproduce

See https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7ade3f344755eb86b20aae39610a5758b7e510d8/packages/flutter_tools/templates/app/lib/main.dart.tmpl#L153

```dart

floatingActionButton: FloatingActionButton(
        onPressed: _incrementCounter,
        tooltip: 'Increment',
        child: const Icon(Icons.add),
      ), // This trailing comma makes auto-formatting nicer for build methods.

```

As far as I can tell this is no longer needed since the new formatter is enabled by default, so the comment should probably be removed. Other usages:

https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aflutter%2Fflutter%20%2F%2F%20This%20trailing%20comma%20makes%20auto-formatting%20nicer%20for%20build%20methods.&type=code

text: Plan Item #175847: DropDownMenu secondary trailing widget (e.g. for clear action) ### Use case If having a [DropDownMenu](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu-class.html) it is currently only possible to replace the `trailingIcon`, which is tied to an action. Internally a TextField with an IconButton is used to provide this dropdown action. For convenience it should also be possible to define more trailing actions apart from the dropdown functionality. In comparison to a TextField, where this trailing icon is usually used as a `clear` action, it should also be possible for a `DropDownMenu` to define such an action via e.g. `secondaryTrailing`, which also could hold a list to define multiple trailing widgets, in front of the current dropdown tralingIcon. ### Proposal How it currently is: Proposal: E.g. by providing a property called `secodaryTrailing` As reference: This is how its shown for a textfield with a clear icon: https://m3.material.io/components/text-fields/overview ### Workarounds For me no workarounds are known. If replacing the `trailingIcon` with a `Row` of `IconButton`s the Row would be still wrapped by the IconButton of the DropDownMenu Widget, which would result in a double wrapped IconButton, if providing a second one next to it. ### Alternative solutions We could also have a `trailing` parameter, which overrides all actions, but then it would be advisable to instead have a `trailingBuilder` to provide the dropdown action in the parameters, so one is able to customize the trailing widget without need of rebuilding the dropdown action.
text_with_context: [PLAN] #175847 | Area: material | Milestone: | Type: plan_item | Reactions: 1 Plan Item #175847: DropDownMenu secondary trailing widget (e.g. for clear action) ### Use case If having a [DropDownMenu](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu-class.html) it is currently only possible to replace the `trailingIcon`, which is tied to an action. Internally a TextField with an IconButton is used to provide this dropdown action. For convenience it should also be possible to define more trailing actions apart from the dropdown functionality. In comparison to a TextField, where this trailing icon is usually used as a `clear` action, it should also be possible for a `DropDownMenu` to define such an action via e.g. `secondaryTrailing`, which also could hold a list to define multiple trailing widgets, in front of the current dropdown tralingIcon. ### Proposal How it currently is: Proposal: E.g. by providing a property called `secodaryTrailing` As reference: This is how its shown for a textfield with a clear icon: https://m3.material.io/components/text-fields/overview ### Workarounds For me no workarounds are known. If replacing the `trailingIcon` with a `Row` of `IconButton`s the Row would be still wrapped by the IconButton of the DropDownMenu Widget, which would result in a double wrapped IconButton, if providing a second one next to it. ### Alternative solutions We could also have a `trailing` parameter, which overrides all actions, but then it would be advisable to instead have a `trailingBuilder` to provide the dropdown action in the parameters, so one is able to customize the trailing widget without need of rebuilding the dropdown action.

text:
Plan Item #176005: Suggest final keyword for new Widget classes by default

### Use case

Developers create new widgets frequently. Many of these widgets are not designed for inheritance.

By suggesting final, we prevent accidental subclassing, making the widget safer and signaling the intended use clearly.


Improves code readability and maintainability by indicating immutability at the class level.

Could potentially optimize performance, as Dart may optimize final classes better in certain scenarios.

### Proposal

Currently, when creating a new Widget class in Flutter, IDEs and templates do not suggest using the final keyword for the class. 

Most Widgets are not intended to be subclassed, and marking them as final improves code safety and can have minor performance benefits.


The proposal is to add a suggestion or template update in Flutter tools and IDEs so that when developers create a new Widget, the final keyword is recommended or auto-inserted.

Plan Item #176113: Support navigating to source locations from stack frames displayed by `WidgetPreviewErrorWidget`

`WidgetPreviewErrorWidget` displays a list of stack frames if a widget throws an exception during initialization. The script URIs for each stack frame should be links that cause connected IDEs to navigate to the specified source location.

text:
Plan Item #176543: Add AlignmentDirectional constants to AlignmentGeometry

### Use case

The `AlignmentGeometry` class now contains redirecting constants like `center` or `topLeft` to allow the usage of dot shorthands:

```dart
Align(
  alignment: .topLeft,
  child: Foo(),
),
```

However, the predefined values for directional alignment (like `topStart`) are missing from the `AlignmentGeometry` class:

```dart
Align(
  // cannot use a dot shorthand
  alignment: AlignmentDirectional.topStart,
  child: Foo(),
),
```

### Proposal

Add the corresponding constants into `AlignmentDirectional` to enable dot shorthands for them as well:

```dart
Align(
  alignment: .topStart,
  child: Foo(),
),
```

Plan Item #176543: Add AlignmentDirectional constants to AlignmentGeometry

### Use case

The `AlignmentGeometry` class now contains redirecting constants like `center` or `topLeft` to allow the usage of dot shorthands:

```dart
Align(
  alignment: .topLeft,
  child: Foo(),
),
```

However, the predefined values for directional alignment (like `topStart`) are missing from the `AlignmentGeometry` class:

```dart
Align(
  // cannot use a dot shorthand
  alignment: AlignmentDirectional.topStart,
  child: Foo(),
),
```

### Proposal

Add the corresponding constants into `AlignmentDirectional` to enable dot shorthands for them as well:

```dart
Align(
  alignment: .topStart,
  child: Foo(),
),
```

text:
Plan Item #176566: ☂️[a11y] Use defaultTargetPlatform instead of platform from the theme for Semantics flags in a few widgets

### Use case

I noticed this @chunhtai's comment at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/173418#discussion_r2274331276:

_"we should check defaultTargetPlatform instead of from the theme, if for some reason someone wants android feels and look on an iOS, the voice over will still be broken"_

Indeed, in case users explicitly configure a specific platform (let's give it as Android for eg), but run on another platforms (let's give it as iOS for eg), accessibility might work incorrectly.
There are a few existing widgets that use platform from the theme to check:

- [x] ExpansionTile: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/177002

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/b95ab963a4ca4137cf665ee35534e38b31b1a4cb/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/expansion_tile.dart#L593

- [x] AppBar: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/177000

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/04d91b1ef8e6a53d33aadbab2d4823ce674a2b71/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/app_bar.dart#L1069

- [x] Dialog: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/177001

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/56d5389456e0049e1ed36c96e365fd255ef27e3a/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/dialog.dart#L774

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/56d5389456e0049e1ed36c96e365fd255ef27e3a/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/dialog.dart#L851

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7100df24f611a8b91669c4d33c63bcdad2448703/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/dialog.dart#L1276

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/56d5389456e0049e1ed36c96e365fd255ef27e3a/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/dialog.dart#L1315

- [x] _PopupMenu: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/177003

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/1cee136823542a2c0c09b6672bdac22724c4279c/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/popup_menu.dart#L1195

- [x] BottomSheet: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/177004

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/a04fb324be734cf18811c30c06baf9bf07b3bab3/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/bottom_sheet.dart#L685

- [x] Drawer: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/177005

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/1280fad73f1979ecb5ce88f1206a1370349dd1dc/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/drawer.dart#L253

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/1280fad73f1979ecb5ce88f1206a1370349dd1dc/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/drawer.dart#L688


### Proposal

Use defaultTargetPlatform instead of using platform from the theme.

Plan Item #176645: Do we need this "The <parameter_name> parameter is required"?

I see multiple phrases of the form "The <parameter_name> parameter is required" in the doc comments, while it is enforced by the compiler using the required keyword, or the parameter is a regular positional parameter.
For example, in packages/flutter/lib/src/material/menu_anchor.dart:
```dart
  /// The [label] parameter is required.
  const MenuAcceleratorLabel(this.label, {super.key, this.builder = defaultLabelBuilder});
```
Do we need these phrases?

Plan Item #176685: Feature request (build): enable engine builds optimized for size

### Use case

As a developer targeting embedded devices, I wish to build the flutter engine optimized for size rather than speed as it is advantageous on some platforms to reduce the binary footprint as much as possible.

### Proposal

Add a flag to the `engine/src/flutter/tools/gn` tool to enable size optimizations, eg

```
/engine/src/flutter/tools/gn --optimize-for-size
```

The flags `--optimize-for-size` and `--unoptimized` should probably be mutually exclusive

text:
Plan Item #176826: Add flutter config --ios-min-version to set default iOS deployment target globally

### Use case

Currently, Flutter does not provide a way to set a global default iOS deployment target across all projects. Developers must manually edit each project’s ios/Podfile or the Xcode project settings to change the line:
```
platform :ios, '12.0'
```

This is repetitive and error-prone when maintaining multiple projects or CI/CD environments.

### Proposal

Add a new global configuration option:

`flutter config --ios-min-version 13.0`

This command would:

- Store the specified iOS minimum deployment target in the Flutter config file (~/.flutter_settings or equivalent).
- Apply the version automatically when creating new projects with flutter create.
- Optionally, update existing projects when running flutter pub get or a new command like flutter fix.

**Reason for proposal**

Many plugins (e.g., Firebase, camera, Google Maps) have raised their minimum iOS version to 13.0 or higher.

Developers often maintain multiple Flutter projects; changing each Podfile manually is tedious.
Setting a global default aligns with existing configuration commands such as:

flutter config --android-sdk
flutter config --enable-<feature>

CI/CD environments and team setups would benefit from consistent deployment targets.


Thus, expected behavior is:
```
flutter config --ios-min-version 13.0
flutter create my_app
```
should generate ios/Podfile with:
platform :ios, '13.0'

Candidate branch: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/tree/flutter-3.38-candidate.0
Dashboard link: https://flutter-dashboard.appspot.com/#/build?repo=flutter&branch=flutter-3.38-candidate.0

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/176746 stamped the new candidate release branch for the 3.38 beta. When this pull request landed, it triggered the `Linux flutter_release_builder`, which [skipped the engine build](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/dart-internal/builders/flutter/Linux%20flutter_release_builder/1055/overview) causing  the `Linux flutter_analyze` test to fail when trying to reference the release engine build in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/176799 and postsubmit failures for the stamping PR itself:

- [`Mac_arm64 verify_binaries_codesigned`](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/flutter/builders/prod/Mac_arm64%20verify_binaries_codesigned/628/overview)
- [`Mac_x64 verify_binaries_codesigned`](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/flutter/builders/prod/Mac_x64%20verify_binaries_codesigned/631/overview)
- [`Linux linux_web_engine_tests`](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/flutter/builders/prod/Linux%20linux_web_engine_tests/4050/overview)

We need a way to trigger an engine build for the release and ideally, a way to avoid this problem in the future. 

Currently blocking https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/176744.

cc @jtmcdole who mentioned this is likely not related to content-aware hashing but rather another optimization
cc @justinmc

Plan Item #176870: Feature request (build): add option to tune for specific CPU

### Use case

As a developer cross-compiling the flutter engine for a specific embedded platform, I want to specify the CPU model in order to get the best performance from the compiled code.

### Proposal

Add a `engine/src/flutter/tools/gn` option `--cpu-tune` or similar that passes a specifc CPU variant through to the compiler's `-mtune` flag.

There is already support for this in the build via the `arm_tune` gn argument in `arm.gni`, for ARM CPUs. It just needs some support from the `gn.py` frontend.

Plan Item #176987: Implement RoundedSuperellipseInputBorder

### Use case

I want to use the squircle corners of iOS for my input fields. There is the `RoundedSuperellipseBorder` to do exactly that, but a `TextField` works only with `InputBorder`s that extend from `ShapeBorder`, which makes those classes sibling classes of `OutlinedBorder` which `RoundedSuperellipseBorder` extends from.

### Proposal

I believe there should be a `RoundedSuperellipseInputBorder` that fills the gap. On iOS native text fields use the same corner math, see the search bar in the contacts app, so this is necessary when aiming for platform respective UI.

Plan Item #177000: [a11y] Use defaultTargetPlatform instead of platform from the theme for AppBar title semantics

### Use case

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/04d91b1ef8e6a53d33aadbab2d4823ce674a2b71/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/app_bar.dart#L1069

In case users explicitly configure a specific platform (let's give it as Android for eg), but run on another platform (let's give it as iOS for eg), accessibility might work incorrectly.

### Proposal

Use defaultTargetPlatform instead of using platform from the theme.

In case users explicitly configure a specific platform (let's give it as Android for eg), but run on another platform (let's give it as iOS for eg), accessibility might work incorrectly.

### Proposal

Use defaultTargetPlatform instead of using platform from the theme.

Plan Item #177057: package:web_benchmarks needs to be updated for unified skwasm/canvaskit renderers

Now that the skwasm and canvaskit renderers emit the same frame events (see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/175392), the `expectedBenchmarkMetrics` method here [1] no longer returns the expected events. They are now be the same for both skwasm and canvaskit.

To handle this, I suggest making `useWasm` an optional named parameter instead of required. 

[1] https://github.com/flutter/packages/blob/1c0be204c45eea42927eb6005bb45f16a8fffb07/packages/web_benchmarks/lib/src/metrics.dart#L152-L155

_(Note: This was noticed because the DevTools benchmark tests to began failing, see https://github.com/flutter/devtools/issues/9456)_

Plan Item #177087: drawerEnableOpenDragGesture property in DrawerThemeData

### Use case

I wanted to disable opening app drawer using gesture cause it conflicts with ios back navigation. I then found that scaffold does have the property `drawerEnableOpenDragGesture` for this exact use case. But the issue is that I have a few seperate pages in my applicaiton and in order to disable the drawer gesture in all pages I am required to define this on each scaffold. It's fine and it would work, but I feel that this is code duplication and unncessacary since we already have a pattern to override app wide default values using ThemeData. But when I checked the `DrawerThemeData` it does not have this option.

### Proposal

I would like to have `drawerEnableOpenDragGesture` property in `DrawerThemeData` so that I can change default behaviour across entire application instead of defining it in each scaffold which is code duplication

Plan Item #177195: Expose iOS "Auto-Play Animated Images" accessibility setting

### Use case

In the Zulip app for iOS, we'd like to check whether the iOS 17+ "Auto-Play Animated Images" setting is enabled, so that when loading an image, we can choose between animated and still versions.

- iOS user doc: https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/reduce-onscreen-motion-iph0b691d3ed/ios
- iOS developer doc: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swiftui/environmentvalues/accessibilityplayanimatedimages



### Proposal

I propose adding a new field to [`AccessibilityFeatures`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/AccessibilityFeatures-class.html), providing this setting's value.

There are already some iOS-only fields, such as [`AccessibilityFeatures.onOffSwitchLabels`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/AccessibilityFeatures/onOffSwitchLabels.html).

Plan Item #177285: update the material_color_utilities dependency version

### Use case

this doesn't impact flutter directly, but, the current `material_color_utilities` dependency is at `0.11.1` while the latest version on pub.dev is `0.13.0` , this is blocking the use of flutter in other dart projects that depend on `material_color_utilities 0.13.0` and also require flutter as a dependency.
As far as i can tell ... there are no breaking changes so it should be fine to update the dependency without issues.

### Proposal

bump the `material_color_utilities` dependency to version `0.13.0` .

text:
Plan Item #177330: Expose iOS "Customize onscreen motion" accessibility settings

_(Replaces #177195, which was about just one setting. This issue covers the whole bundle of related settings.)_

### Use case

In the Zulip app for iOS, we'd like to check whether the iOS 17+ "Auto-Play Animated Images" setting is enabled, so that when loading an image, we can choose between animated and still versions.

- iOS user doc: https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/reduce-onscreen-motion-iph0b691d3ed/ios
- iOS developer doc: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swiftui/environmentvalues/accessibilityplayanimatedimages

### Proposal

I propose adding new fields to [`AccessibilityFeatures`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/AccessibilityFeatures-class.html), providing "Auto-Play Animated Images" and, for completeness, all the related settings in that [Apple user doc](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/reduce-onscreen-motion-iph0b691d3ed/ios):

- [x] Reduce Motion _(already exposed, as [`AccessibilityFeatures.reduceMotion`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/AccessibilityFeatures/reduceMotion.html))_
- [ ] Vehicle Motion Cues
- [ ] Dim Flashing Lights
- [ ] Auto-Play Animated Images _(for Zulip's use case; was tracked in #177195)_
- [ ] Auto-Play Video Previews
- [ ] Auto-Play Message Effects
- [ ] Prefer Non-Blinking Cursor
- [ ] Limit Frame Rate

We would follow the existing pattern of iOS-only fields, such as [`AccessibilityFeatures.onOffSwitchLabels`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/AccessibilityFeatures/onOffSwitchLabels.html), by saying in the dartdocs "Only supported on iOS."

Completion: 1/8 tasks done

text: Plan Item #177479: Improve error message on string matching We should improve the error message on string matching in flutter_test. We have been looking at how to best roll in updates to date localizations that have (in a few cases) updated typical whitespace (U+0020) to be NBSP (non breaking space, U+202F). The localizations team confirmed this is WAI, and in some locales (like French) is a required typography rule. We have found users' might experience this update like this: Which would be super frustrating to debug! We should improve the error message here to specify what the whitespace character is in the error message. Separately we also discussed that in some cases (maybe not this particular case) users might like a test method that allows them to match strings without attention paid to whitespace, tracked in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/177478 A little bit more context on this from the localizations team is available in b/442629033
text_with_context: [PLAN] #177479 | Area: testing | Milestone: | Type: plan_item | Reactions: 0 Plan Item #177479: Improve error message on string matching We should improve the error message on string matching in flutter_test. We have been looking at how to best roll in updates to date localizations that have (in a few cases) updated typical whitespace (U+0020) to be NBSP (non breaking space, U+202F). The localizations team confirmed this is WAI, and in some locales (like French) is a required typography rule. We have found users' might experience this update like this: Which would be super frustrating to debug! We should improve the error message here to specify what the whitespace character is in the error message. Separately we also discussed that in some cases (maybe not this particular case) users might like a test method that allows them to match strings without attention paid to whitespace, tracked in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/177478 A little bit more context on this from the localizations team is available in b/442629033

text:
Plan Item #177670: [Flutter web-semantics] Feature request: Enable Accessibility button upper left 1 pixel instead of offscreen 1 pixel

### Use case

Automation tools such as Maestro and probably Appium seem to not be able to tap on
`      document.querySelector('flt-semantics-placeholder[aria-label="Enable accessibility"]')
                .click();` because it is at absolute positivion -1 left and -1 top of the screen (offscreen).

Maestro does appear to be able to tap on an invisible button with no text if it is at 1,1.

I am not sure if i have bandwidth to PR this but i wanted to get the discussion going.
See: https://docs.flutter.dev/ui/accessibility/web-accessibility#invisible-button

Can anyone think of a reason that the ('flt-semantics-placeholder[aria-label="Enable accessibility"]' has to be offscreen rather than "just" onepixel, invisible, transparent etc?


### Proposal

Change just 2 characters in the code to make the button be "+" 1 left and "+" one top insteda of "-" (or remove the negative)

Plan Item #177723: [pigeon] Consider generating singletons for @EventChannelApi

### Use case

Hello, I love how pigeon deals with the complexity of native channels, however, due to my unfamiliarity with the topic I made a mistake of calling the generated method for creating an EventChannel stream, thus breaking platform communication with previous instances. I understand that this might be a problem that some Plugin code must deal with, but this also seems a pretty general problem. So, consider generating code that will ensure only one instance of the EventChannel exists. I am currently using this pattern that I found in a plugin that I use:

https://github.com/llfbandit/app_links/blob/e99efa708dde9886e0982d9b53b7b6b05e807c31/app_links/lib/src/app_links.dart#L52-L86

Here a StreamController is used that encapsulates the EventChannel stream and makes sure only one exists and also closing it when no subscribers are left.


### Proposal

I propose that the class that is annotated with `@EventChannelApi()` is used as the singleton class name and that methods or getters in this class create the needed StreamControllers.

Plan Item #177799: Migrate Expansible animation properties to AnimationStyle for a less broad API surface

### Use case

[Expansible](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Expansible-class.html) has animation properties [Expansible.curve](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Expansible/curve.html), [Expansible.reverseCurve](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Expansible/reverseCurve.html) and [Expansible.duration](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Expansible/duration.html). These properties are encapsulated in [AnimationStyle](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/animation/AnimationStyle-class.html).

### Proposal

Deprecate these properties and replace them with a single `animationStyle` property.

### Use case

[Expansible](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Expansible-class.html) has animation properties [Expansible.curve](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Expansible/curve.html), [Expansible.reverseCurve](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Expansible/reverseCurve.html) and [Expansible.duration](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Expansible/duration.html). These properties are encapsulated in [AnimationStyle](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/animation/AnimationStyle-class.html).

### Proposal

Deprecate these properties and replace them with a single `animationStyle` property.

Plan Item #177886: Update Java version in flutter create

### Use case

When you run flutter create, default Java version is 11 - check `android/app/build.gradle.kts`. I'm sure whether this is for backwards compatibility or something else, but I think this can be update to the latest, or at least more recent Java version

### Proposal

Update Java version to 21.

Plan Item #178003: Make generic paths look better on platforms without MSAA.

### Use case

On platforms without MSAA, the tessellated paths we render look very choppy (see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/167181).

### Proposal

This can be alleviated by using SDFs to render generic paths. We've done the same with circles (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/177482) and lines (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/164734). Any other solution that makes generic paths look nicer will also do.

This will require changes to `Canvas::DrawPath` https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/e5d5c01850f2d33106f00a19017682f09e83e48b/engine/src/flutter/impeller/display_list/canvas.cc#L327

This can be alleviated by using SDFs to render generic paths. We've done the same with circles (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/177482) and lines (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/164734). Any other solution that makes generic paths look nicer will also do.

text:
Plan Item #178009: Allow DropdownMenu to be non-editable and focusable (select control) ### Use case A `DropdownMenu` configured as a non-editable dropdown (select control) should not allow text input in its text field, regardless of how it receives focus. When focused, the component as a whole should be highlighted, allowing the user to open the menu with keyboard actions such as the `Space` or `Enter` key. This behavior would align with standard dropdown components, such as the [Material Web select component](https://material-web.dev/components/select/#usage). Right now, it is not possible to have a non-editable dropdown and also provide a `FocusNode`. To clarify, by non-editable dropdown I mean a dropdown that works like a normal select control without a text field, only a selector. In many real situations we still need to use a `FocusNode` object, for example: 1. When submitting the previous field and programmatically moving focus to the dropdown. 2. When submitting a form and focusing the first invalid field, which might be the dropdown. --- **Example of current behavior:**
Details To create a `DropdownMenu` that functions as a simple, non-editable selector, the current approach involves setting `requestFocusOnTap: false`, `enableSearch: false`, and `enableFilter: false`. While this correctly prevents the text field from activating on a mouse click, it does not prevent keyboard input when the widget gains focus through other means. In this example, pressing the button gives focus to the `DropdownMenu`, which incorrectly allows text input. Flutter version: ```text stable : 3.35.7 master : 3.38.0-1.0.pre-407 ``` ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() => runApp(const MyApp()); class MyApp extends StatefulWidget { const MyApp({super.key}); @override State createState() => _MyAppState(); } class _MyAppState extends State { @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp( home: Scaffold( body: Container( padding: const EdgeInsets.all(24.0), alignment: Alignment.center, child: Column( mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center, spacing: 24, children: [ OutlinedButton( onPressed: () => focusNode.requestFocus(), child: const Text('Focus DropdownMenu'), ), DropdownMenu
### Proposal #### Proposal 1 If [enableFilter](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/enableFilter.html) is set to `false` and [enableSearch](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/enableSearch.html) is also set to `false`, the widget should behave like a select control without a text field, regardless of how focus is requested or whether a custom `FocusNode` is provided. It should not depend on `requestFocusOnTap` for that behavior. The [requestFocusOnTap](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/requestFocusOnTap.html) parameter should be removed. --- Keep in mind that if we choose this proposal, we must also consider another scenario that is currently not implemented: **When `DropdownMenu` is used as a select, it should focus/highlight the item that matches keyboard input even when there is no text field.** This matters because the current [enableSearch](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/enableSearch.html) setting disables highlight behavior when set to `false`, which could conflict with this new behavior if implemented in the future.
Example **Case 1** 1. Open dropdown 2. Press **"a"** (Go to the first item that starts with "a") 3. Press **"a"** again (Go to the next item that starts with "a") 4. Press **"a"** again (Return to the first item that starts with "a" since there are no more matches) **Case 2** 2. Quickly type a combination of keys like `"a4"` — this focuses the item that matches that prefix. In this case it jumps to `a4` and skips `a2` and `a3`.
--- #### Proposal 2 Add another parameter to make the component work strictly as a select. Possible names: * `showSearch`: `bool` * `searchable` : `bool` * `selectOnly`: `bool` * `enableTextFieldSearch`: `bool` * `mode`: `enum DropdownMenuMode {select, textField}` The [requestFocusOnTap](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/requestFocusOnTap.html) parameter should be removed. In this case, make sure the current [enableSearch](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/enableSearch.html) does not cause confusion with this new parameter.

In order to address the TASK_BOT died errors, we downgraded from Ubuntu 24 to Ubuntu 22. This rolled out slowly on Nov 4th and uncovered some test failures on the dashboard. These failures appear around many of the Android tests which do not include ninja / cmake / clang in their dependencies list of ci.yaml.

Updating multiple locations in: #178054 

```
          {"dependency": "clang", "version": "git_revision:5d5aba78dbbee75508f01bcaa69aedb2ab79065a"},
          {"dependency": "cmake", "version": "build_id:8787856497187628321"},
          {"dependency": "ninja", "version": "version:1.9.0"}
```

This has the tools installed in the cache on the CI machines and appear to fix the issue.

Outstanding issue: [Linux web_skwasm_tests_2](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/flutter/builders/try/Linux%20web_skwasm_tests_2) appears to be failing with a chrome conneciton.

text: Plan Item #178071: Please collect anonymized build-time histograms Currently the `flutter` tool does not collect build-time data (afaict). Based on my conversations with Shorebird customers and some minimal (anonymized) histogram sharing from at least one popular flutter build provider, long-tail build times in the wild are much worse than I realized. We had one customer who has had (for months!) 3hour flutter build times. We have another who reports 2 hour flutter build times. These are outliers, I'm sure. But we don't know how bad it is because we don't have good data. Google is uniquely positioned to collect (and respond to) some data here. Here is an anonymized histogram from this provider earlier this year. This was just a tiny sample of their builds, so I'm sure it's not fully representative. But even this, shows a very long tail distribution. My guess is that this is maybe ~1d of work in the Flutter tool. Sharing this kind of data (bucketed total time of execution) is something we already report via dart analytics for the analyzer, and should be well within existing analytics agreements. The only work here should be actually making the change to flutter_tool. I'd recommend reporting bucketed-by-minute. If you'd like to see information about what Chrome does (who has 1000+ analytics like this), they have lots of docs: https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+/lkgr/tools/metrics/histograms/README.md

We had one customer who has had (for months!) 3hour flutter build times. We have another who reports 2 hour flutter build times. These are outliers, I'm sure. But even this, shows a very long tail distribution. My guess is that this is maybe ~1d of work in the Flutter tool. Sharing this kind of data (bucketed total time of execution) is something we already report via dart analytics for the analyzer, and should be well within existing analytics agreements. The only work here should be actually making the change to flutter_tool. I'd recommend reporting bucketed-by-minute. If you'd like to see information about what Chrome does (who has 1000+ analytics like this), they have lots of docs: https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+/lkgr/tools/metrics/histograms/README.md

text:
Plan Item #178154: Improve navigation order for Screenreaders when using Slivers to serve Material Guidelines

### Use case

[According to Material Design, the **focus order of elements** in a Scaffold should be AppBar -> Navigation Bar -> FAB -> Content](https://m3.material.io/components/floating-action-button/accessibility#c9dfe413-7661-43f5-988b-bce6da2bc47e)

This is easy to do as long **as your AppBar is in the Scaffold's appbar slot as now appBar, body and fab are siblings**. You can just set the semantics sortKey and the screenreader will navigate accordingly.

However, when it comes to slivers, the AppBar is part of the body's CustomScrollView.
You will have to scroll through the entire body to get to the fab. That's very bad user experience. Maybe I am missing something, because this seems to be quite a fundamental problem with how sliver pages are built.

There shouldn't be the need for some hacky solution to this.

```Scaffold(
      body: CustomScrollView(
        slivers: [
          SliverAppBar(
            title: const Text('My Title'),
          ),
          SliverList(
            delegate: SliverChildBuilderDelegate(
                  (context, index) => ListTile(
                    title: Text('Entry $index'),
                  ),
              childCount: 20,
            ),
          ),
        ],
      ),
      floatingActionButton: FloatingActionButton(
        onPressed: () {},
        tooltip: "Add To List",
        child: const Icon(Icons.add),
      ),
    );
```

### Proposal

The best solution would be to be able to set the order across the entire screen and not just siblings.
